model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The schedule change already improved optimization. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With a fixed 256-step budget, increasing batch size exposes the model to substantially more training examples per run. That is a simple, low-risk way to improve early-budget learning for this MLP without adding architectural complexity.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The larger batch made better use of the fixed step budget. A higher peak learning rate is the cleanest next adjustment because OneCycle and AdamW can usually tolerate a modest LR increase after batch scaling, potentially improving progress within 256 steps.","secondary_modes":["summaries","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.1","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The current setup is optimizing well, so the next likely gain is better generalization rather than more raw speed. Mild dropout plus label smoothing is a compact regularization package that often lowers validation loss for overparameterized MLP classifiers on CIFAR-10.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.1","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The previous regularization package clearly hurt validation loss. Reverting those two changes is the highest-value next step because it restores the stronger optimization regime before trying more targeted adjustments.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The strongest gain so far came from using more examples per optimizer step. Pushing batch size to 256 is the most direct continuation of that trend, giving the model near-full-pass coverage of the training set within the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The best gains came from scaling the effective optimization budget with larger batches, and the previous LR increase also helped. With batch size now at 256, the next clean experiment is a moderate peak-LR increase to push OneCycle/AdamW harder within the same 256 steps.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The last step slightly worsened validation loss, so the best next move is to revert the peak learning rate to the stronger 3e-3 setting and preserve the batch-256 gain before exploring other directions.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The clearest pattern in this run is that larger batches materially improve fixed-step performance by covering more training examples. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The dominant pattern in the search is that increasing batch size consistently lowers validation loss by giving the model much broader data coverage within the fixed 256-step budget. The next clean test is to continue that scaling trend to 1024 while keeping the rest of the winning setup intact.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Batch scaling has been the dominant source of gains, and the current batch size is now much larger than the one where 4e-3 last underperformed. {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"At batch size 1024, increasing the peak learning rate to 4e-3 slightly degraded validation loss. Reverting to the stronger 3e-3 setting is the safest next move before spending more steps on scheduler or architecture changes.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        pct_start=0.05,","old":"        pct_start=0.1,","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"With only 256 steps, a 10% OneCycle warmup may spend too many updates below the most useful learning-rate range. {"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        final_div_factor=100.0,","old":"        final_div_factor=25.0,","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The current fixed-step setup is close to a good solution, so a small end-of-training schedule refinement is more justified than a disruptive change. {"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        final_div_factor=25.0,","old":"        final_div_factor=100.0,","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"A more aggressive final decay slightly hurt validation loss, so the best next step is to restore the stronger 25.0 setting and preserve the current best schedule.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The batch and schedule seem close to saturated, so the next useful low-complexity probe is regularization strength. {"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The reduced weight decay was effectively neutral to slightly worse, so with one step left the highest-value move is to restore the exact best-known regularization setting and finish on the strongest verified configuration seen in this run.","secondary_modes":["summaries","micro"]}
